goal: 修复 context retrieval eval 中 superseded-decision 和 docs-only-intent 的召回缺口

summary.title: Context retrieval surfaces superseded lineage and anti_patterns
summary.what: Fixes query-mode context retrieval so it no longer misses constraints in the eval catalog. Query candidates now union every keyword hit, keep recent weak-signal intents that the JSON scorer could surface, and expand explicit supersession lineage when a returned intent replaces older decisions. The scorer and SQLite query index now include summary what/why plus anti_patterns what/why, so hard constraints can drive retrieval instead of only being displayed after selection.
summary.why: The eval failures showed two product-level reliability gaps: agents could see the replacement Parquet export decision without the superseded CSV lineage, and documentation anti_patterns such as the AGENTS.md terminology rule were invisible to retrieval. Those misses undermine the intent-first claim because an agent cannot respect a constraint it never receives.
summary.user_goal: 修复 context retrieval eval 中 superseded-decision 和 docs-only-intent 的召回缺口

decisions.chose: Union all query keyword hits and append recent weak-signal intents before scoring.
decisions.rationale: Using only the alphabetically first keyword let SQLite prefiltering change retrieval semantics; recent weak signals were part of the JSON scorer's behavior and must not disappear when the cache is present.

decisions.chose: Search and score anti_patterns alongside title, goal, summary, decisions, and risks.
decisions.rationale: Anti-patterns are hard constraints; treating them as display-only made docs-only and safety-rule intents unretrievable.

decisions.chose: When a superseder is returned, include the intents it supersedes and apply supersession as a post-sort ordering constraint.
decisions.rationale: Decision lineage is explicit metadata and should bypass ordinary keyword thresholding once the replacement is relevant.

rejected.reason: The fixtures encode real retrieval expectations; changing them would hide product gaps instead of fixing recall.
Keeping the index useful is still valuable; broadening indexed surfaces plus adding recent candidates preserves correctness while retaining pruning on larger views.

risks: Query-mode retrieval can include more recent weak-signal intents than the keyword index alone would return; very large repos may need later tuning if this becomes a latency issue.
Older SQLite cache files do not have the new summary_what and summary_why columns, so ReadIntentViewsByQuery will fall back to the JSON view until the cache is rebuilt.

anti_patterns.what: Using a single keyword as the complete context --query candidate filter
anti_patterns.why: The first keyword may be incidental and can exclude intents that the in-memory scorer or explicit lineage would otherwise surface.

anti_patterns.what: Treating anti_patterns as display-only retrieval payload
anti_patterns.why: Anti-patterns are hard constraints; if they do not participate in candidate selection and scoring, agents can miss the exact rules they are expected not to violate.

anti_patterns.what: Enforcing supersession only by score adjustment before sorting
anti_patterns.why: Rounding, thresholding, and chained replacements can still hide or reorder decision lineage; supersession must be an explicit result-set and ordering rule.

followups: If query latency grows on large repos, tune the recent weak-signal window or add a bounded recent index while preserving JSON-scan semantics.

architectural_claims: context --query cache prefiltering must preserve the set of intents the JSON scorer can meaningfully surface
anti_patterns are first-class retrieval relevance signals
supersession is explicit decision lineage and is expanded after initial relevance matching
behavioral_changes: context --query searches summary what/why and anti_pattern what/why through SQLite-backed query lookup
context retrieval includes superseded intents when their superseder is returned
supersession ordering is enforced after relevance sorting
mainline eval run now passes all eight embedded fixtures

data_model_changes.name: mainline SQLite cache intents table
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Derived cache is rebuilt from the JSON MainlineView; old cache query failures fall back to JSON until rebuild.

migration_notes: The SQLite cache is derived and rebuilt from the JSON MainlineView; stale old-schema cache reads fall back to JSON on query error until rebuild.
